parentQuestion: Glands whose secretions are stored and then released when the cell ruptures and ...

scaffolds.q: Glands can release their secretions in different ways.
scaffolds.explain: Accept this reality:drawn into this — different glands have VERY different delivery methods. some use careful exocytosis (like handing packages through a window), some tear off part of themselves, and some... well, some go out with a BANG. Wait for it.

scaffolds.q: Sebaceous glands produce oily secretions for the skin and hair.
scaffolds.explain: Sebaceous glands make sebum — that's the oil that keeps your skin and hair lubricated. But The plot thickens beautifully here:: the cell literally fills itself with lipid until it's so bloated it RUPTURES. Travel deeper into this: on this — the cell IS the product.
